name: Chukotka Autonomous Okrug (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the Bering Sea, the Chukchi Sea, and the Arctic sky
the Arctic snows, ice, and the polar landscape
the courage and labour of the people of Chukotka; ties to Russia
the polar sun on the horizon

description: A blue field with a white isoceles triangle along the hoist (its base on the hoist edge, its apex pointing toward the fly); within the triangle, a yellow ring surrounds a small horizontal white-blue-red bar.
history: Adopted on 18 May 1994 by the Duma of the Chukotka Autonomous Okrug.

features.symbolism: the blue field stands for the Bering and Chukchi Seas around Chukotka
a white isoceles pile whose base lies along the hoist edge and apex extends past the centre toward the fly; represents the Arctic snows and ice of Chukotka
a thick yellow ring within the hoist triangle, representing the polar sun.
a small white-blue-red horizontal bar inside the yellow ring evokes the flag of Russia and the okrug's place within the Russian Federation.
